seo.title: Building CLI tool development patterns with TypeScript an...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for CLI tool development patterns with TypeScript and Rust.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to building a CLI tool with TypeScript, focusing on cross-platform compatibility, error handling, and integration patterns. Follow these steps to implement a production-ready developer tool.

steps.title: Initialize project structure
steps.explanation: Create a new project directory and configure TypeScript with a tsconfig.json file. Install required dependencies including commander and cross-env for platform-specific commands.

code.content:
mkdir devtool-cli && cd $_
npm init -y
tsconfig.json
npm install --save commander cross-env

pitfalls: Forgetting to add 'type': 'module' in package.json for ES modules
Incorrect TypeScript target configuration causing runtime errors

steps.title: Define CLI command structure
steps.explanation: Create a main.ts file that initializes the CLI with commander. Define base commands and subcommands with appropriate options and descriptions.
pitfalls: Not using .parse() to trigger command execution

steps.title: Implement platform-specific logic
steps.explanation: Use the os and path modules to handle platform-specific file paths and command execution. Add environment checks for Windows/MacOS/Linux.
pitfalls: Hardcoding file paths instead of using platform-aware modules
Ignoring environment variable differences between OSes

steps.title: Add error handling patterns
steps.explanation: Implement centralized error handling with try/catch blocks. Create custom error classes for different failure scenarios.
pitfalls: Not differentiating between user errors and system errors

steps.title: Create test suite
steps.explanation: Write unit tests for core commands using Jest. Test command parsing, error conditions, and platform-specific behavior.
pitfalls: Missing edge case testing for invalid inputs

steps.title: Package for distribution
steps.explanation: Configure npm scripts for building and publishing. Create a .npmignore file to exclude development files.
pitfalls: Including source files in the published package
Forgetting to update the version field before publishing

content.conclusion: By following these steps, you've created a CLI tool with robust error handling, cross-platform support, and test coverage. Focus on maintaining clear command hierarchies and consistent error messaging to improve developer experience.
